_provenance.reviewed_by: mainframe-modernization CoE — partner team; audit against AWS Migration & Modernization + APN blog posts 2020-2026. Added reference_urls[] per tool with verified canonical URLs from KB frontmatter.

sources.label: AWS blog: Mainframe Replatform with Rocket Software (2025-12-12)
AWS blog: Reimagining with AWS Transform + Claude Code (2026-05-08)

rocket_enterprise_server.name: Rocket Enterprise Server (formerly Micro Focus Enterprise Server)

reference_urls.title: Mainframe Replatform: Best Practices with Rocket Software on AWS (2025-12-12)
Proactive Rocket Software Licensing Daemon Management on AWS (2026-05-11)

rocket_enterprise_server.runtime: Self-managed on EC2 / ECS / EKS. AWS M2 Managed Runtime (Micro Focus branding) is closed to new customers as of 2025-11-07 — existing customers only.
strengths: Highest-fidelity CICS/JCL emulation on AWS
AWS's featured replatform partner as of 2025-12-12 (per AWS blog)
Mature tooling; largest installed base among FSI
Supported via AWS-Rocket partnership: Solution Architecture & Design Documentation (SA&DD), Functional Design Specification (FDS), Migration Feasibility Assessment (MFA)
weaknesses: License cost scales with workload; can be high for large estates
Emulation preserves monolith shape — no path to cloud-native without additional refactor
New customers no longer eligible for AWS M2 Managed Runtime; must self-manage on EC2 or partner-managed
rocket_enterprise_server.fsi_considerations: Well-accepted by FSI auditors; supports FIPS 140-2 cryptographic modules. Clear control mapping for SOX/FFIEC. Rocket has deep FSI relationships from the acquired Micro Focus book.
rocket_enterprise_server.historical_note: This entry was previously named 'Micro Focus Enterprise Server (OpenText)'. Rocket Software acquired the Micro Focus mainframe modernization business from OpenText in mid-2024. AWS's positioning shifted materially: the M2 Managed Runtime (Micro Focus flavor) closed to new customers 2025-11-07, and Rocket is now the featured replatform partner per the 2025-12-12 blog.

aws_transform_for_mainframe.name: AWS Transform for mainframe

reference_urls.title: Transform Enterprise Workloads up to 4x Faster with Agentic AI — AWS Transform GA (2025-05-15)
Reimagining Mainframe Applications with AWS Transform and Claude Code (2026-05-08)
Accelerate Mainframe Modernization with AWS Transform — A Comprehensive Refactor Approach (2025-12-22)
Agentic AI Meets PL/I — Modernizing Mainframes with AWS Transform (2026-04-10)

aws_transform_for_mainframe.runtime: Runs on AWS; output targets Java (Spring Boot) + PostgreSQL / Groovy on ECS/Fargate or Lambda
strengths: GA since 2025-05-15; AWS-native billing and support
Agentic AI reverse-engineers business rules with Given/When/Then acceptance criteria
Two patterns supported: Automated Refactor (preserve monolith, modernize language) and Reimagine (decompose to microservices — pair with Claude Code for forward engineering)
Deterministic output for Refactor; agentic + HITL for Reimagine
Customer-reported metrics: Itaú ~90% time reduction on discovery, Visma ~55% annual cost savings, 4x faster than manual refactor
weaknesses: Newest product in the space (GA 2025-05-15) — smaller reference base than Rocket or Blu Age standalone
Reimagine pattern requires Claude Code as the forward-engineering companion — not a single-vendor solution
Very large portfolios (10M+ LOC) need decomposition strategy first — see 'Tackling large mainframe portfolio' blog for methodology
aws_transform_for_mainframe.fsi_considerations: AWS-native controls (IAM, KMS, CloudTrail) apply to the transformation service itself and to the refactored output. FSI customers using PL/I (banking/insurance/public sector) get specific support per 2026-04-10 blog.
aws_transform_for_mainframe.typical_timeline: 6-18 months (Refactor pattern); 18-36 months (Reimagine pattern with Claude Code)
aws_transform_for_mainframe.historical_note: AWS Transform for mainframe consolidates the capability previously delivered as 'Amazon Q Developer Transform capability for mainframe' (re:Invent 2024 preview) which itself built on 'AWS Blu Age'. GA'd 2025-05-15 as one of three AWS Transform agents alongside .NET and VMware.

aws_blu_age.name: AWS Blu Age (now part of AWS Transform for mainframe)

reference_urls.title: Automated Refactoring from Mainframe to Serverless Functions and Containers with AWS Blu Age (2020-02-11)
Automate the Building, Testing, and Deployment Processes of AWS Mainframe Modernization with AWS Blu Age (2024-09-27)

aws_blu_age.runtime: AWS M2 Blu Age runtime (Java on ECS/Fargate) remains for existing workloads. New Blu Age transformation engagements route through AWS Transform for mainframe as of GA 2025-05-15.
strengths: Automated COBOL-to-Java refactor; cloud-native output
AWS-owned; integrated billing and support
Blu Age *runtime* (Java on ECS/Fargate) remains the target-side execution environment for AWS-Transform-refactored code
Good for customers wanting long-term cloud-native posture
weaknesses: Standalone AWS Blu Age transformation is largely superseded by AWS Transform for mainframe (GA 2025-05-15)
Refactor adds risk vs rehost; regression surface is larger
Best fit for medium-complexity estates; very large estates need phased approach
aws_blu_age.fsi_considerations: Refactored Java code inherits AWS-native controls (IAM, KMS, CloudTrail). Useful for DORA operational-resilience evidence.
aws_blu_age.historical_note: AWS Blu Age was previously the standalone AWS-owned automated-refactor tool. Its transformation capability is now delivered via AWS Transform for mainframe (see the `aws_transform_for_mainframe` entry). The Blu Age runtime (Java target execution on ECS/Fargate) remains a legitimate target-side entry point.

heirloom.name: Heirloom (Heirloom Computing)
heirloom.runtime: Java EE on any JVM (EKS, ECS, EC2)
strengths: Fast compile-to-Java cycle; good dev-loop ergonomics
Runs on standard JVM; no runtime lock-in
Strong CICS BMS screen conversion
weaknesses: Smaller ecosystem / partner network
IMS coverage limited
heirloom.fsi_considerations: Compatible with standard Java security frameworks; requires customer-built audit evidence since no AWS-managed runtime.

astadia.name: Astadia

reference_urls.title: Modernize Mainframe Workloads to AWS with Astadia Automated Refactoring (2022-07-13)

strengths: Deep Unisys and niche-mainframe experience
Strong on Natural/ADABAS and IDMS estates most partners avoid
Delivery-led; predictable outcomes
weaknesses: Partner-delivered rather than product-only — cost depends on engagement
Less AWS-native integration than Blu Age
astadia.fsi_considerations: Long track record with FSI clients; produces standard Java output auditable with AWS controls.

ntt_unikix.name: NTT UniKix

reference_urls.title: How to Modernize a Replatformed Mainframe Development Lifecycle with AWS and NTT DATA (2021-08-02)

strengths: Strong CICS runtime fidelity
Lower license cost than Micro Focus at scale
weaknesses: Smaller ecosystem
Manual ops tooling — no AWS-managed runtime
ntt_unikix.fsi_considerations: Customer-managed runtime; customer owns all audit-evidence tooling.

lzlabs.name: LzLabs Software-Defined Mainframe
strengths: Supports HLASM — rare among partners
Binary-compatible: minimal code change for rehost
weaknesses: Legal history (IBM litigation) — FSI customers may require extra review
Niche vendor; smaller support footprint
lzlabs.fsi_considerations: Perform vendor legal/risk due-diligence before commit. Strong fit when HLASM volume is material.

kyndryl.name: Kyndryl Mainframe Modernization Services

reference_urls.title: Accelerating Mainframe Modernization — How Partners are Leveraging AWS Transform (2025-05-15)

strengths: Largest mainframe services practice globally
Deep FSI and regulated-industry experience
Tool-agnostic: will pair with Micro Focus, Blu Age, or Heirloom
weaknesses: Services-heavy: time-and-materials cost model
Quality depends heavily on assigned team
kyndryl.fsi_considerations: Strong at navigating FSI regulator relationships; useful when regulator engagement is in scope.

dxc.name: DXC Technology

reference_urls.title: Accelerating Mainframe Modernization — How Partners are Leveraging AWS Transform (2025-05-15)

strengths: Large delivery capacity
Incumbent at many large FSI customers
weaknesses: Services-heavy
Consistency varies by geography
dxc.fsi_considerations: Often already a customer vendor; leverage existing contractual relationship.

tcs_mastercraft.name: TCS MasterCraft TransformPlus
strengths: Strong in APAC and EMEA
Tight tool + delivery integration
weaknesses: Tool and services bundled — less flexibility
Output is standard Java; cloud-nativeness depends on post-refactor work
tcs_mastercraft.fsi_considerations: Common in APAC FSI; TCS usually holds end-to-end accountability.

precisely.name: Precisely (Syncsort)
strengths: Best-in-class mainframe data ingestion to AWS
EBCDIC/packed-decimal fluency
Integrates with Kafka/MSK, Kinesis, S3
weaknesses: Data-only — does not address compute modernization
precisely.fsi_considerations: Strong fidelity on numeric conversion (packed decimal, comp-3) — important for payments and ledger accuracy.
